assertions.title: Dashboard loads with real data from /portal/dashboard

steps.action: Login with test credentials and navigate to /admin
steps.expected: GET /api/v1/portal/dashboard called, 4 stat cards with non-empty label, value, optional detail
steps.observed: GET /api/v1/portal/dashboard called and returned 200. Only 3 stat cards rendered (Revenue run rate: ₹1180.00, Fulfilment SLA: 0.0%, Dealer coverage: 2). API returns only 3 highlights; 4th card does not exist in API response.

steps.action: Check for undefined/null values and skeleton placeholders
steps.expected: No undefined, null, or skeleton placeholders after 5s
steps.observed: No undefined, null, or skeleton placeholders found. All 3 rendered cards have valid label, value, and detail.

assertions.issues: Only 3 stat cards rendered instead of required 4. Backend API returns only 3 highlight items. The frontend code uses highlights.slice(0, 4) so it correctly renders up to 4, but the backend only provides 3.

assertions.title: Stat cards render exactly 4 highlight metrics

steps.expected: Exactly 4 stat cards in a responsive grid, each with sparkline SVG, label, and value
steps.observed: Only 3 stat cards rendered: Revenue run rate (₹1180.00), Fulfilment SLA (0.0%), Dealer coverage (2). Grid uses 'grid grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4' which supports 4 cards, but only 3 data-testid=stat-card elements exist.

steps.action: Verify each card has sparkline SVG, label, and value
steps.expected: Each card has decorative sparkline SVG, label, and value
steps.observed: All 3 rendered cards have sparkline SVG (viewBox=0 0 100 100 with path elements), label, and value. Sparklines use decorative shapes with gradient fills.

assertions.issues: Only 3 stat cards rendered instead of 4. Backend API returns 3 highlights. The grid layout class correctly supports 4 columns at lg breakpoint.

assertions.title: Dashboard displays correct greeting and date

steps.expected: Time-of-day greeting matching current hour
steps.observed: Greeting shows 'Good morning' — current hour is 6 (before 12), which is correct for morning greeting.

steps.expected: Today's date displayed
steps.observed: Date shows 'Tuesday, 31 March 2026' matching current date.

steps.expected: Subtitle reads 'Platform overview and operational status.'
steps.observed: Subtitle reads 'Platform overview and operational status.'

assertions.title: Quick access cards navigate to correct pages

steps.expected: 6 cards: Users, Roles, Approvals, Audit Trail, Changelog, Settings
steps.observed: 6 quick-access cards present: Users, Roles, Approvals, Audit Trail, Changelog, Settings — each with description text.

steps.expected: Navigate to /admin/users
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3002/admin/users

steps.expected: Navigate to /admin/roles
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3002/admin/roles

steps.expected: Navigate to /admin/approvals
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3002/admin/approvals

steps.expected: Navigate to /admin/audit-trail
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3002/admin/audit-trail

steps.expected: Navigate to /admin/changelog
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3002/admin/changelog

steps.expected: Navigate to /admin/settings
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3002/admin/settings

assertions.title: Dashboard error state shows retry button

steps.expected: Error banner with 'Failed to load dashboard data.' and Retry button
steps.observed: Dashboard loads successfully (API returns 200). Cannot simulate API failure in live environment without network interception.

steps.action: Review error state code in AdminDashboardPage.tsx
steps.expected: Error UI exists with AlertCircle icon, error text, and Retry button
steps.observed: Code review confirms error state exists: role='alert' div with AlertCircle icon, error message, and Retry button with onClick={load}. Error handling is implemented correctly in code.

assertions.issues: Cannot simulate API failure in live environment without network interception. The error state code exists and looks correct but could not be tested against a real failing API.

assertions.title: No console errors on dashboard load

steps.action: Clear errors, reload /admin, wait for full load, check console errors
steps.expected: Zero console.error entries after full load
steps.observed: agent-browser errors command returned no output (empty). No console.error entries found. Console shows only debug/info/warning messages from Vite HMR and React Router future flags.

evidence.consoleErrors: none (no console.error entries; only debug [vite], info [React DevTools], and warning [React Router future flags])

assertions.title: Dashboard responsive layout at all breakpoints

steps.expected: Stat cards in 2-col grid, quick-access single column, sidebar hidden, hamburger visible
steps.observed: Stat cards in 2-col grid (grid-cols-2), quick-access single column (grid-cols-1), sidebar hidden (class 'hidden lg:block'), hamburger visible ('Open menu' button found). No horizontal scroll (scrollWidth=375, clientWidth=375).

steps.expected: 2-col grids, sidebar hidden
steps.observed: Stat cards in 2-col grid (grid-cols-2, lg breakpoint not reached), quick-access in 2-col grid (sm:grid-cols-2 active), sidebar hidden (display: none for hidden lg:block), hamburger visible ('Open menu' button found). No horizontal scroll.

steps.expected: 4-col stat grid, sidebar visible
steps.observed: Stat grid class includes 'lg:grid-cols-4' (4-column layout active), sidebar visible (display: block for lg:block), only 3 stat cards present (API limitation, not layout). No horizontal scroll (scrollWidth=1440, clientWidth=1440).

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 4 passed, 2 failed, 1 blocked. VAL-DASH-001 and VAL-DASH-002 fail because the backend API returns only 3 highlight items instead of the required 4. The frontend correctly renders all available data and supports a 4-column grid layout, but the backend only provides 3 metrics (Revenue run rate, Fulfilment SLA, Dealer coverage). VAL-DASH-005 is blocked because the dashboard API always returns 200 — error state cannot be triggered without network interception. All other assertions pass: greeting and date are correct, all 6 quick access cards navigate correctly, no console errors, and responsive layout works at all breakpoints.
